quotes.text: Debugging is like being a detective in a crime movie where you're also the suspect.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every programmer has a story about the time they spent 8 hours debugging a missing semicolon. If they say they don't, they're lying.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: My code doesn't have bugs. It has 'random features.'
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: A programmer's wife tells him: 'Go to the store and buy a loaf of bread. If they have eggs, buy a dozen.' He comes home with 12 loaves.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best thing about a Boolean is even if you are wrong, you are only off by a bit.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Hardware: the parts of a computer system that can be kicked.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The Internet? Is that thing still around?
quotes.author: Homer Simpson

quotes.text: The best error message is the one you never see. The second best is one that makes you laugh.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Maybe I’m taking crazy pills, but I’m still stuck on “why the hell does a TUI need to run in terminal React by way of JavaScript” The fact that Anthropic felt the need to buy a runtime so they could make their TUI better speaks more to the quality of engineering than anything else IMO. If rewrites are so easy, why not rewrite CC in a native language? Would’ve been a hell of a lot cheaper.
quotes.author: weakfish
quotes.source: Hacker News — Claude Code uses Bun written in Rust now
quotes.extraction_note: Humorous critique of unnecessary complexity in software toolchains, with a grug-brained call for simplicity.
